consistency.general: The feature name, class, and level are consistent with established D&D 5e conventions for core class features (Evasion for Rogue at level 7).
consistency.terminology: Uses standard D&D terminology ('Dexterity saving throw', 'half damage', 'no damage').

quality.clarity: The description is clear and directly states the mechanical benefit: turning a successful Dex save for half damage into no damage, and a failed Dex save for full damage into half damage.

mechanics.trigger: Subjected to an effect that allows a Dexterity saving throw to take only half damage (e.g., Fireball, Dragon Breath).
mechanics.effect: If save succeeds: take 0 damage (normally half damage). If save fails: take half damage (normally full damage).
mechanics.balance_assessment: This feature is very powerful and is a signature ability for the Rogue class at this level. It provides near-immunity to damaging area effects that rely on Dexterity saves, which is highly thematic for a Rogue.

analysis.notes: The feature references an external element ('_ice storm_](#ice-storm)'), suggesting it was likely extracted from a system that handles cross-referencing (like D&D Beyond or a similar SRD database).
